mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate near zero reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks versus cash, producing a structural tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean forward return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-style acceleration in unemployment, where UNRATE's 3-month average rises 0.5 percentage points or more above its trailing 12-month low, signals the onset of a labor-market driven recession that compresses forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises >=0.5pp above trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances where the Sahm-style UNRATE trigger fires, or the signal's hit rate is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, this joint confirmation of consumer and residential investment demand signals broad-based economic strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0 AND HOUST MoM growth > 0 concurrently
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST positive growth signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in Rank IC by any margin over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and near-term corporate earnings momentum, which is quickly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and 5d forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate rises faster than headline CPI inflation, the resulting rise in real interest rates raises the equity risk premium and discount rate simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this effect only manifests when both series interact to produce a positive and rising real rate.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate turns positive and rising for 3+ consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) during periods of rising positive real rates, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS), where month-over-month job gains fall below the trailing 6-month average by a material margin, signals weakening labor demand that precedes reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change falls more than 1 standard deviation below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in over 55% of triggered payroll-deceleration instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects an ongoing economic expansion phase that supports corporate earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive relationship (Rank IC < 0.03) with trailing GDPC1 growth over any 3-year rolling window, or negative GDP growth quarters fail to coincide with below-average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) year-over-year growth turns negative during a late-cycle expansion with elevated unemployment, this signals an emerging industrial recession that is not yet fully priced by equity markets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets adjust.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following negative INDPRO YoY readings during late-cycle regimes are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the effect disappears when tested outside late-cycle unemployment conditions.
